questions.q: In which year was the legislation now known as MGNREGA enacted?
questions.explanation: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act was enacted in 2005.

questions.q: MGNREGA guarantees how many days of wage employment in a year to a rural household?
questions.explanation: The Act guarantees up to 100 days of wage employment per year to a rural household.

questions.q: MGNREGA is named after which national figure?
questions.explanation: The Act is named the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.

questions.q: MGNREGA primarily provides employment to which category of households?
questions.explanation: The Act guarantees wage employment to rural households whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.

questions.q: MGNREGA is best described as which type of guarantee?
questions.explanation: MGNREGA is often described as embodying a legal right to work for rural households.

questions.q: The kind of work primarily offered under MGNREGA is:
questions.explanation: MGNREGA provides unskilled manual work, typically on public works such as water conservation and rural roads.

questions.q: Which local self-government body plays a central role in planning and implementing MGNREGA works?
questions.explanation: Gram Panchayats have a central role in planning and implementing works under MGNREGA.

questions.q: MGNREGA is one of the world's largest programmes of what kind?
questions.explanation: MGNREGA is regarded as one of the largest public employment programmes in the world.

questions.q: A document recording the entitlement of a registered MGNREGA household is the:
questions.explanation: Registered households are issued a job card recording their entitlement under MGNREGA.

questions.q: MGNREGA falls primarily under the administrative responsibility of which Union ministry?
questions.explanation: MGNREGA is administered by the Union Ministry of Rural Development.

questions.q: Under what name was the Act originally enacted before being renamed after Mahatma Gandhi in 2009?
questions.explanation: It was originally the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA), renamed MGNREGA in 2009.

questions.q: Under MGNREGA, if employment is not provided within a stipulated period of demand, the applicant is entitled to:
questions.explanation: If work is not provided within the stipulated time, the applicant is entitled to an unemployment allowance.

questions.q: Within how many days of applying must employment ordinarily be provided under MGNREGA?
questions.explanation: Employment is to be provided within 15 days of application, failing which an unemployment allowance is payable.

questions.q: MGNREGA requires that a minimum share of beneficiaries be women; that share is at least:
questions.explanation: The Act provides that at least one-third of the beneficiaries should be women.

questions.q: A key social accountability mechanism mandated under MGNREGA to review works at the village level is the:
questions.explanation: MGNREGA mandates regular social audits of works, conducted with community participation.

questions.q: Under MGNREGA, if a worksite is located beyond a certain distance from the worker's residence, the worker is entitled to:
options: An additional travel and subsistence allowance
questions.explanation: If the worksite is beyond the prescribed distance, an extra allowance toward travel and subsistence is payable.

questions.q: A large share of works under MGNREGA has traditionally focused on which area?
questions.explanation: Permissible works include water conservation, drought proofing, land development and rural connectivity.

questions.q: MGNREGA wage payments are now largely routed through which mechanism to improve transparency?
options: Cash handed out at the worksite
Direct credit to bank or post office accounts
questions.explanation: Wages are generally paid by direct transfer to workers' bank or post office accounts.

questions.q: Demand for work under MGNREGA is described as:
options: Allotted only once a year by officials
questions.explanation: MGNREGA is a demand-driven programme in which eligible workers apply for employment when they need it.

questions.q: MGNREGA seeks to limit the use of which input on its worksites to maximise labour employment?
questions.explanation: The Act discourages the use of contractors and labour-displacing machinery to maximise employment generation.

questions.q: The body the Act provides for to advise the central government on MGNREGA is the:
questions.explanation: The Act establishes a Central Employment Guarantee Council to advise on implementation and monitoring.

questions.q: MGNREGA's employment guarantee is provided at the level of the:
questions.explanation: The 100-day guarantee applies to each rural household rather than to each individual.

questions.q: MGNREGA was initially rolled out in February 2006 covering how many districts in its first phase?
questions.explanation: The programme was launched in February 2006 in 200 districts in its first phase, before being extended nationwide.

questions.q: By which financial year had MGNREGA been extended to cover essentially all rural districts of the country?
questions.explanation: MGNREGA was extended to cover all rural districts of the country by 2008-09.

questions.q: Under MGNREGA, the power to notify the applicable wage rate for the scheme rests with the:
questions.explanation: The central government notifies the MGNREGA wage rates, which it revises from time to time.

questions.q: The assembly of village residents that approves the shelf of works and conducts social audits under MGNREGA is the:
questions.explanation: The Gram Sabha recommends works and is central to the social audit process under MGNREGA.

questions.q: MGNREGA provides that wages be paid within a stipulated period of work completion, failing which the worker may be entitled to:
options: A criminal complaint against the village
A doubling of the work guarantee
questions.explanation: The Act and its rules provide for compensation to workers for delays in the payment of wages.

questions.q: An official designated to receive and redress grievances under MGNREGA at the district or block level is commonly known as the:
questions.explanation: MGNREGA guidelines provide for an Ombudsman to receive and dispose of grievances related to the scheme.

questions.q: Funding for the wage component of MGNREGA is borne primarily by which level of government?
questions.explanation: The central government bears the full cost of the unskilled wage component, with costs shared on other items.

questions.q: The original NREGA legislation that became MGNREGA received the assent of the President in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act received presidential assent in September 2005.
